steps.title: Find the proportion of sample voters supporting Candidate A
steps.content:
From the table, voters for Candidate A: 202 (female) + 34 (male) = 236 out of 400 total sampled.

Proportion: $$\frac{236}{400} = \frac{59}{100}$$

steps.title: Scale the sample proportion to the town's total voters
steps.content:
Apply the sample proportion to the town's 6,000 voters:
$$\frac{59}{100} \times 6,000 = 59 \times 60 = 3,540$$
The estimate is 3,540 voters for Candidate A.
